===={{aniseries|JN}}====
Wikstrom made his main series debut in ''[[JN056|Searching for Chivalry!]]''. {{Ash}} and [[Goh]] traveled to [[Kalos]] to participate in a training camp hosted by Wikstrom at the [[Castle of Chivalry]]. Wikstrom had the boys traverse through a variety of obstacle courses while wearing armor and defending themselves from Wikstrom's Pokémon. Ash and Goh eventually completed all of the challenges and earned Knight Medals for both themselves and their Pokémon. He also helped Goh evolve his {{p|Scyther}} into {{TP|Goh|Scizor}} by giving it a [[Metal Coat]] and transferring it through a [[trade machine]].

In ''[[JN060|Beyond Chivalry… Aiming to be a Leek Master!]]'', [[Rinto]] revealed that he also trained at the Castle of Chivalry. During Rinto's battle with Ash, Ash used some of what he learned from Wikstrom to be able to win the battle.

In ''[[JN103|The Future is Now, Thanks to Strategy!]]'', Wikstrom was briefly seen battling [[Drasna]] at the Castle of Chivalry. In ''[[JN104|Taking Two For The Team!]]'', he met Ash prior to his [[World Coronation Series]] battle against Drasna in [[Lumiose City]] and saw that Ash's {{p|Farfetch'd}} had evolved into {{AP|Sirfetch'd}} since their last meeting. He later watched the battle and congratulated Ash after he had won it.

In ''[[JN130|Toying With Your Motions!]]'', Wikstrom watched Ash's match against {{an|Leon}} in the [[Masters Eight Tournament]] finals, shedding tears at witnessing Sirfetch'd's chivalrous performance.
